card_description: George Lucas is a historical figure referenced in Philip K. Dick's Exegesis. Appears in 2 Exegesis passages. Context: "In a profound pivot, Dick interprets his entire life and 3-74 experience through the lens of George Lucas's *Star Wars*."

linked_segments.summary: In a profound pivot, Dick interprets his entire life and 3-74 experience through the lens of George Lucas's *Star Wars*. He identifies Christ as "Ben Kenobi" (a dead but omnipresent guide), himself as
linked_segments.title: 1976-09-15_Dorothy_64.txt

linked_segments.summary: Dick identifies a startling 1:1 correspondence between his 1967 story "Faith of Our Fathers" and his 3-74 experiences. He concludes that the Xerox letter was a "phony test/trap" (using "party jargon")
linked_segments.title: 1976-09-15_Dorothy_74.txt
